DECLARATION OF JEFFREY PETTWAY IN SUPPORT OF MOTION FOR RELIEF FROM DEFAULT (TRANSACTION ID # 100016473) FILED BY DEFENDANT PETTWAY, JEFFREY AN INDIVIDUAL POITRAS, AMY

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS JEFFREY PETTWAY AND AMY POITRAS' MOTION FOR RELIEF FROM DEFAULT IS GRANTED. THE MOTION IS TIMELY AND PROVIDES SUFFICIENT SUPPORT FOR DISCRETIONARY RELIEF PER CCP 473. MOREOVER, CALIFORNIA'S STRONG PUBLIC POLICY OF DECIDING CASES ON THEIR MERITS FAVORS THE GRANTING OF THE MOTION. PLAINTIFF MICHAEL HARMELL'S ARGUMENT THAT THE MOTION SHOULD BE DENIED BECAUSE THERE IS NO MERIT TO DEFENDANTS' DEFENSES TO THE COMPLAINT ITSELF LACKS MERIT AND IS CONTRARY TO WELL-ESTABLISHED LAW THAT A MOTION FOR RELIEF FROM DEFAULT IS NOT THE APPROPRIATE FORUM TO ADJUDICATE THE MERITS OF A CLAIM OR A DEFENSE THERETO. THE PREVAILING PARTY IS TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER THAT TRACKS THE TENTATIVE RULING VERBATIM, SHOW COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312 AND DELIVER A HARD COPY OF THE PROPOSED ORDER TO DEPT. 302 FOR PROCESSING. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN. CLERK: ROSIE NOGUERA. REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER, CSR #5976.

ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar May-05-2016 Continued To Jun-21-16 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Relief From Default
Off Calendar Apr-26-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance was made by any Party, Counsel or Interested Person.

The Court confirmed its tentative ruling. DEFENDANTS JEFFREY PETTWAY and AMY POITRAS' Motion For Relief From Default is granted. The motion is timely and provides sufficient support for discretionary relief per CCP 473. Moreover, California's strong public policy of deciding cases on their merits favors the granting of the motion. Plaintiff Michael Harmell's argument that the motion should be denied because there is no merit to defendants' defenses to the complaint itself lacks merit and is contrary to well-established law that a motion for relief from default is not the appropriate forum to adjudicate the merits of a claim or a defense thereto. The prevailing party is to prepare a form of order that tracks the tentative ruling verbatim, show compliance with CRC 3.1312 and deliver a hard copy of the proposed order to Dept. 302 for processing.

Judge: Harold Kahn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976. (302/HK)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Mar-07-2016 Set For Order To Show Cause On May-10-16 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610
